goal: Fix CI after explicit signal queue cleanup

summary.title: Fix explicit signal CI regressions
summary.what: Aligned context retrieval, lifecycle fixtures, hub inherited-hotspot fixtures, and lint cleanups with the explicit signal queue contract after the signal-rule PRs merged.
summary.why: CI was still exercising legacy summary risks, follow-ups, and anti-patterns as active signals even though the new product contract keeps those fields legacy-only and uses explicit signal commands for durable queues.
summary.user_goal: Fix CI after explicit signal queue cleanup

decisions.chose: Score only explicit open risks and follow-ups in context query mode.
decisions.rationale: This preserves useful retrieval for active queues without letting legacy summary.risks, summary.followups, or summary.anti_patterns drive default context.

decisions.chose: Move failing tests to explicit risks and explicit constraints where active signal behavior is expected.
decisions.rationale: The tests now dogfood the new write rules instead of reintroducing old seal-summary behavior.

decisions.chose: Remove dead signal helper parameters and unused status counters instead of preserving compatibility shims with no callers.
decisions.rationale: The unused shims were only serving the old legacy-surface implementation and were blocking CI lint.

rejected.alternative: Restore scoring for legacy summary risks, follow-ups, or anti-patterns.
rejected.reason: That would make old noisy seal fields active again and conflict with the explicit-only signal queue design.

review_notes: Validated the originally failing engine and hub tests directly, then ran the affected engine/hub/cli packages and the pinned golangci-lint v1.62.2 command.

architectural_claims: Default context retrieval keeps legacy action-signal fields wire-compatible but non-active.
Explicit open risk and follow-up records are indexed in memory for query relevance without populating legacy context fields.
behavioral_changes: Query mode can retrieve an intent by matching explicit open risk or follow-up text.
Query mode continues to ignore legacy anti-pattern-only matches and no longer expects CJK fallback to score via anti_pattern.
Full lifecycle test seal payloads avoid durable action signal fields and use review_notes for non-propagating context.
